output.summary: Add .neon-green gradient override to the generic gradient system in colors.css, following the existing pattern used by .blue, .yellow, .orange, .purple, and .green.

investigation.currentGradientStructure: The generic gradient system in colors.css (lines 143-172) defines :root defaults for --gradient-left (var(--accent-color)) and --gradient-right (var(--color-blue)), then per-accent-color overrides for .blue, .yellow, .orange, .purple, and .green. Each override sets --gradient-left and --gradient-right to a complementary color pair. The .neon-green class is missing from the gradient system, even though it exists as an accent color class (line 73-79) with its own --accent-color overrides.
investigation.neonGreenGradientPair: Based on the old hero-specific code that defined .hero.neon-green: --gradient-left = var(--color-neon-green), --gradient-right = var(--color-blue). This matches the :root defaults since neon-green is the default accent color, but an explicit .neon-green class is needed for when neon-green is applied as an override on a page that has a different default accent color.
investigation.whyNeeded: Without an explicit .neon-green gradient override, a page with a non-neon-green default accent that has a .neon-green section would get the wrong gradient colors. The .neon-green class correctly sets --accent-color, but --gradient-left and --gradient-right remain set to whatever the page default is. For example, on a .blue page, a .neon-green hero would get gradient-left=blue, gradient-right=neon-green instead of gradient-left=neon-green, gradient-right=blue.

fileChanges.description: Add .neon-green gradient override to the generic gradient system
